Week 4 - challenge completed.

entry.title: Re: The Data Ownership dilemma — and how SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products helps resolve It
entry.content:
How is SAP supposed to know exactly what our business processes look like
or what specific data the business unit needs ?
This only works for a specific, standardized area, such as the finance department.
Therefore, all the data products must be adjusted so that the business unit’s questions can be answered.
The adjustment will be handled through SAP, or IT will take care of it (again).

entry.title: Capacity responsible planner group in work center
entry.content: I know that it is currently not possible to configure new capacity responsible in capacity header for work center but in our case no standard entry exists in table and hence we are unable to create any work center. The capacity category is mandatory and once it is entered system directly navigates to header where capacity responsible in mandatory. I was able to do it on customizing tenant but on Test tenant there seems to be no entry available in Table TC27. Attaching screenshot for reference:

entry.title: Re: CAP Initializr: Generate SAP CAP Projects in Seconds

entry.title: How to transport Native mobile app create using BTP SDK for Android
entry.content:
I have create a Mobile App using BTP SDK for Andriod.

Now I want to transport it to PROD subaccount. 
Is there a way, i cannot find any help document ?
Or we can only do it using export/import only ?

entry.title: Re: Setup and Activation Guide: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Thanks for this question.

Here are my finidings : 

When you have BTP Joule configured in one subaccount/region and need to use the Connectivity service from a different subaccount or region, you'll need to establish proper trust relationships and configure destinations to enable secure communication across these boundaries.

Steps for implementation

1. Setup Trust Between Subaccounts

2. Configure Destination in Source Subaccount

3. Cross-Region Authentication Setup

Please have a look at this recently released guide https://help.sap.com/doc/de3af3c0f81642dbaa4d36172ed57a72/CLOUD/en-US/79bfc83ab386450c8cd9c7937ce26a3a.pdf

If the specific scenario is not covered then you can reach out to our product support as we may not have released a specific document for each scenario.

entry.title: 4g group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing sap s/4 hana public cloud for a customer who has operations in China and Malaysia. As for organization structure, it has 2 company codes for Chinese entities and 1 company code for Malaysia entity.

DEV-Customizing system is provisioned.  CNAP accounting principle is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scopse item 5W2 is activated in initial scoping when deploying DEV-Customizing system. 4G group valuation ledger is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to handle this situation what actions we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Hi, Week 4 - Completed, Challenge Completed.

Package: ZAC_154774

entry.title: Re: RAP Event: consume SAP standard event and trigger custom RAP event
entry.content:
Thank you very much for this @hironobu_nakayama, it works perfectly for my usecase (filter WBS element create /change events and add some data to the payload).

Only thing I'd point out is there is a missing IMPORTING keyword in the syntax given on this blog:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

Once I added the IMPORTING keyword, it works:

CLASS lcl_event_consump DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_event_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS:
      consume_changed FOR ENTITY EVENT IMPORTING changed_instances FOR purchaseorder~changed.
ENDCLASS.

 Nonetheless, great blog that opens so many possibilities for clean core development. Kudos!

entry.title: Re: How to integrate an Adyen terminal into SAP Customer Checkout
entry.content: Unfortunately Adyen require a ridiculous amount of credit card spend per annum for you to qualify to use their terminals!

entry.title: Re: Credit Exposure Mismatch
entry.content: In fact, we can view the details of the Business Partner's Credit Exposure in the app "Display Credit Exposure," which includes multiple company codes rather than just a single one.

entry.title: 4g group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are implementing sap s/4 hana public cloud for a customer who has operations in China and Malaysia. As for organization structure, it has 2 company codes for Chinese entities and 1 company code for Malaysia entity.

DEV-Customizing system is provisioned.  CNAP accounting principle is assigned to leading ledger 0L.  Scopse item 5W2 is activated in initial scoping when deploying DEV-Customizing system. 4G group valuation ledger is auto generated with the activation of 5W2.  Now there is error because of  inconsistency in configuration of ledger.  Pls suggest how to handle this situation what actions we should take now . Thank you! 

Error message: 

Configuration settings need to be corrected.
Message no. FINS_ACDOC_CUST201

Diagnosis
There are inconsistencies in the configuration settings for ledgers.

System Response
As long as configuration settings are inconsistent, postings are not allowed to prevent database mismatches.

When accessing the Financial Statement Versions app, the following warning message appeared:

 

Warning  “This app is outdated and is going to be removed in an upcoming release. Please switch to the successor configuration activity, Define Financial Statement Versions, in Global Hierarchies.”

 

We would like to know when this app is scheduled to be removed, and by when we need to complete the transition. 

Thank you for your time and assistance.

entry.title: Re: DSD Settlement
entry.content: DSD (Direct Store Delivery) settlement in SAP is the process of reconciling and finalizing financial transactions between the delivery vehicle (sales representative) and the company after goods are delivered directly to retail stores. It ensures that all sales, returns, and payments collected during the route are properly accounted for and posted in SAP. Configuration involves setting up routes, settlement profiles, and integration with FI/SD/MM.

entry.title: Re: Remove or deactivate PP/DS relevance for a large set...
entry.content: For your case (300k materials, one-time deactivation), the most practical approach is a custom ABAP program using BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEDATA, executed in background jobs. MASS can work but may struggle with performance at this scale. BRF+ is great if you already use MDG and need governance.

entry.title: Re: MM Status for "Intl Trade: Import" and "Intl Trade: E...
entry.content: Hello Friend, Thank you for the response. However, the Foreign Trade (FT) status group is not visible in the M_MATE_STA authorization group.

entry.title: Re: Spool is not generating for the parent HU
entry.content: In /SCWM/PACK, output determination for HU printing is triggered by specific HU steps/events (like creation, assignment, or changes). You mentioned that the condition record is maintained for HU step “U” (Update). When you add a child HU, the parent HU is updated, and the system recognizes this as a trigger → spool is generated. When you remove a child HU, the system may not classify this as a relevant “update” event for the parent HU, depending on customizing. As a result, no output is triggered. You will need to adjust output determination customizing so that “removal of child HU” is included as a trigger for parent HU printing.

entry.title: Re: Week 4 : Use Joule Predictive Code Completion to Implement Determination
entry.content:
Week 4 completed

Package: ZAC_105

entry.title: Re: PU Withdrawn qty exceeded by 1 EA : 700000367 YES1
entry.content:
This error often comes from duplicate posting attempts or extra HU quantity. Even if PO = 1 and GR = 1, if EWM thinks 2 were withdrawn (due to HU content or task confirmation), ERP will reject it.

The quickest diagnostic is to run /SCWM/PRDI for the inbound delivery and compare line item quantities with the PO in ME23N. That will show you where the mismatch lies.

your explanation was clear, easy to understand, and very well done. Great job!

entry.title: Re: Batch Determination Enhancement with Shelf-Life Fresh...
entry.content: You can configure batch search strategies with shelf-life characteristics, link them to customer channel fields in BP master, and enforce a minimum freshness threshold for domestic orders. Other channels continue with FIFO. The flexibility comes from maintaining the threshold administratively.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Fiori launchpad コンテンツ - 基本シナリオを試してみた
entry.content: 👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍👍

entry.title: Re: How combine different PO's into one delivery using GN...
entry.content:
Yes, the function module GN_DELIVERY_CREATE can create a single inbound delivery that contains items from multiple purchase orders, provided those POs meet the system’s grouping criteria. By default, inbound deliveries are created per PO, but SAP allows delivery combination if customizing and parameters are set correctly.

Inbound deliveries can be combined if:

  • Same vendor (supplier).
  • Same delivery date.
  • Same plant / receiving location.
  • Same shipping conditions.

These checks are controlled in customizing for inbound delivery creation

Maintain a delivery creation profile that allows combining inbound deliveries. Ensure vendor master and PO header data are consistent (same supplier, same incoterms, same plant). Call GN_DELIVERY_CREATE with all relevant PO items in the input structure.

entry.title: Optional Task: Use Joule CDS Unit Test Generation
entry.content:
Thank you to everyone who participated in the ABAP Developer Challenge over the past four weeks and successfully completed all four tasks focused on ABAP AI capabilities. We truly appreciate the enthusiasm and engagement shown throughout the challenge. As a follow-up, we are excited to introduce an additional bonus task.

In Week 2, you enhanced a CDS data definition by implementing simple logic. Building on that work, your next step is to create an ABAP test class to validate the logic within the CDS data definition. For this task, you will utilize another feature of Joule’s ABAP AI capabilities — CDS Test Generation.

Task:

  • Select the CDS data definition that you enhanced in Week 2.
  • Generate an ABAP test class using Joule’s CDS Test Generation feature.
  • Execute the test class and ensure that all generated test cases run successfully.

Please refer to the link below for detailed guidance on how to use this ABAP AI capability.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/use-cds-test-class-generator?locale=en-US

entry.title: TRM – AT210 configured but Tax Flow and Interest Flow still posted into separate FI documents
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

I am currently working on a scenario in SAP Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) where I would like to post interest flow and tax flow into the same FI document using AT210 functionality.

System Context
Module: Treasury and Risk Management (TRM)
Product Type: 55B
Transaction Type: 200
Posting via: TBB1


What I have configured:
- AT210 (V_AT210) mapping
- Source Update Type: MM2203 (Interest)
- Target Update Type: MM1906 (Tax)
- Tax Code: N0
- Condition Type: BASB
- Company Code and Product Type maintained correctly
- Tax flow: MM1906 is generated as a derived flow from MM2203
Both flows have the same payment date
Tax flow appears correctly in the cash flow tab


Issue:
When I execute posting via TBB1:

- MM2203 (interest) is posted into one FI document
- MM1906 (tax) is posted into a separate FI document
They are not merged into a single FI document, although AT210 is maintained.

What additional conditions are required for AT210 to merge tax flow and base flow into a single FI document?
Any guidance or clarification would be highly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Kind regards,
Andromeda.

entry.title: Re: Cannot access the Subprocess Output Parameter in BPA
entry.content:
Hello msiblyb,
In SAP BPA, subprocess output parameters can’t be accessed from within an Automation step because the subprocess is executed as a separate job when triggered from an automation.
To use its output, you need to call the subprocess directly from the Main Process as an independent step. Only then can the Process capture the outputs and pass them to the subsequent steps.
Hope this helps. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Cannot access the Subprocess Output Parameter in BPA
entry.content:
Hello msiblyb,
In SAP BPA, subprocess output parameters can’t be accessed from within an Automation step because the subprocess is executed as a separate job when triggered from an automation.
To use its output, you need to call the subprocess directly from the Main Process as an independent step. Only then can the Process capture the outputs and pass them to the subsequent steps.
Hope this helps. Thanks!

entry.title: GRC Tuesdays: Why Move to SAP GRC for SAP HANA
entry.content:
What is SAP GRC for HANA?

 

Put simply: SAP GRC for SAP HANA is a new evolution of SAP GRC solutions and your path to a future proof GRC platform.

Fully integrated into the SAP Business Suite, SAP GRC for SAP HANA creates a single continuously updated platform, automatically monitoring your core processes, governing access and protecting sensitive data:

 

What is new in SAP GRC edition for HANA?

 

From user experience enhancements with new dedicated Fiori apps, to new capabilities, integration extensions and AI native features, a lot of improvements have already been released in the SAP GRC for SAP HANA version and more are planned.

For details on what is already available, please see the dedicated blog post GRC Tuesdays: What's New in SAP GRC for SAP HANA, released on March 17th 2026.

 

What are the benefits of SAP GRC edition for GRC HANA?

 

In addition to the enhancements delivered, there are also many other reasons to move to this new version, including:

Technical gains

 

More flexibility

Added flexibility fuelled by additional configurability and extended integration capabilities to SAP and non-SAP systems.

 

Easier deployment and maintenance

A unified suite with one software solution and one UI stack provides easier deployment – OnPremise or in cloud – and reduced ongoing maintenance complexity.

 

Ongoing compatibility

This new version, but also all the future ones, keep pace with operating systems, browsers, hardware, and integrations so the product continues to work well in a changing ecosystem.

 

Functional benefits

 

Better user experience

Unified and configurable usability across the entire GRC platform and, more widely, across SAP solutions.

 

Truly multilingual

28 languages are supported across all modules including: Arabic, Chinese (simplified), Traditional Chinese, Croatian, Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, Finnish, French, German, Hebrew, Hungarian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese (Brazil), Romanian, Russian, Serbian, Slovak, Slovenian, Spanish, Swedish, Turkish, Ukrainian

 

Real‑time analytics

 

Intuitive analytical pages for key insights and augmented decision making thanks to the HANA database capabilities.

 

New capabilities

SAP GRC for SAP HANA is not just a new “skin”, it comes with a flurry of enhancements available to this version only and some are detailed in the blog GRC Tuesdays: What's New in SAP GRC for SAP HANA

Just as an excerpt, new features include:

o   Streamlined access role management

o   Flexible workflow management for controls and risks

o   New risk analysis type

o   Shared recommendations for different findings

o   Centralized data protection configurations

o   Alerting notifications when specific data is accessed

o   … and much, much more!

 

Native data protection

 

Centralized attribute-based access control thanks to now embedded UI data protection logging and masking capabilities.

 

Embedded AI

AI functionalities across solutions across all components of SAP GRC for HANA enabling:

o   Easier audit report creation with Generative AI

o   Regulation analysis and summarization with Large Language Modelling

o   Intelligent continuous control monitoring error analysis and resolution

o   Automated sensitive data identification for masking with AI

o   ... and much more!

 

Operational advantages

 

Strategic alignment

The new suite is fully rebuilt on SAP S/4HANA Foundation and SAP HANA, aligning with SAP's long-term tech roadmap. Moving to SAP GRC for SAP HANA aligns organizations with SAP’s broader S/4HANA and HANA‑centric roadmap, making it the recommended path for customers planning long‑term on‑premise or hybrid GRC landscapes.

 

Defined upgrade and migration path

Predefined upgrade paths for SAP GRC "for S/4HANA" customers and seamless migration for SAP GRC "classic" customers.

 

Included in your maintenance

SAP GRC for SAP HANA is already included in the maintenance fee for customers licensed on SAP GRC for S/4HANA modules.

 

A roadmap for the future

 

Futureproof platform

This new version protects your existing investment with a standard mainstream maintenance until 2040 accompanied by a long-term roadmap vision with enhancements regularly delivered until then.

 

Is there a cost associated to this license?

 

Since SAP GRC for SAP HANA is the new version of SAP GRC for SAP S/4HANA solutions, it is included in the standard maintenance for these products and there is no new material number or new commercialization. Organizations licensed for SAP GRC for S/4HANA solutions (such as SAP Access Control for S/4HANA for instance) and up to date on their maintenance will therefore be able to upgrade to the SAP GRC for SAP HANA version with no additional license fee.

Customers licensed on “classic” versions of these products will need to convert their licenses to the “S/4HANA” versions first to be able to benefit from this new SAP GRC for SAP HANA version. To do so, SAP Account Teams will be able to support and guide through the process.

 

How can I migrate to SAP GRC edition for SAP HANA?

 

Upgrade guides are already available from the individual product pages – also accessible in the SAP Help Portal for SAP GRC for SAP HANA:

 

  • Upgrade Guide for SAP Access Control for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP Process Control for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP Risk Management for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP Audit Management for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP Business Integrity Screening for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP UI Data Protection Logging for SAP HANA
  • Upgrade Guide for SAP UI Data Protection Masking for SAP HANA

 

When will it be available?

 

The Release to Customer for SAP GRC for SAP HANA – the initial availability to early adopters, has already happened on March 6th and General Availability, which is when the product is available to all customers, is planned for Q3/2026.

 

What’s next for this new platform?

 

As its name suggests, SAP GRC for SAP HANA is only the “2026” release. With mainstream maintenance currently planned until 2040 and continuous improvement cycles until then, many more enhancements but also use cases will be added:

 

As a matter of fact, the roadmap for each product is regularly updated as new enhancements are planned and it is published on the Elevate your governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) strategy page of the SAP Road Map Explorer tool. Bookmark this page to stay updated.

And if you are interested in learning more about SAP solutions for Governance, Risk, and Compliance, feel free to fill-in the demo request form!

entry.title: API連携で契約マスタが有効化できない
entry.content:
SAPのAPIを使用して契約マスタの登録を行いたいのですが、
登録された契約マスタが無効化となってしまいます。
API連携で有効化するにはどうしたら良いか確認させて下さい。

API連携で使用している項目は添付資料を参照ください。

entry.title: Hi, My name is NUNE VARUN KUMAR. I am pursuing final year B.Tech in CIET College.I
entry.content: I’m currently working on an SAP PP (Production Planning) Implementation Project where I am gaining hands-on experience in production and manufacturing processes. Through this project, I am learning how real business requirements are analyzed and configured in SAP, including areas like Bill of Materials (BOM), Routing, Work Centers, Production Planning, and Material Requirements Planning (MRP).This experience is helping me understand how organizations plan, manage, and execute their production processes efficiently, ensuring optimal utilization of resources and timely delivery of products. I am also gaining insights into shop floor control, production orders, and capacity planning within SAP systems.I’m passionate about continuously improving my knowledge in SAP and understanding how technology supports efficient production planning and operational decision-making in organizations.

entry.title: Re: End-to-End Automatic Order to Billing Process in SAP SD (Low-Coding Approach)
entry.content:
You have covered the OTC process but still, many Business face lot of issues in areas where you missed to explain how it has been taken care in this automation process. So my questions are as under. Probably, these questions will help the community members to take the inputs from this blog

1. Immediate Delivery & ATP
When “Immediate Delivery” is enabled in VOV8, how do you ensure a delivery is created only when quantity is ATP confirmed? Which scopes / checking rules are used to avoid creating deliveries on unconfirmed / future receipts? Also, if sales order have some incompletion log, still the automatic delivery would be created?

2. No Unrestricted Stock / PGI failure
If there is no unrestricted stock and PGI via WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2 fails, what is the user visible outcome and reprocessing path (NAST protocol, RSNAST00 scheduling)? Who monitors and retries failed items?

3. Credit Management interaction
Which credit groups (order/delivery/PGI) are active, and how does the automation behave when a document is credit blocked under FSCM Credit Management (e.g., is delivery/output suppressed until released)?

4. Output requirements under credit block
Have you implemented an output requirement (VOFM) so the delivery output that triggers billing is not determined while credit status ≠ released?

5. Output type confirmation
Please confirm the output type and application used to trigger the PGI→Billing program (you mention REA0). How are condition records (VV21/NACE) set so it only fires for eligible deliveries?

6. PGI technicals
For WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE_2, which flags (e.g., WABUC, SYNCHRON) and error handling via PROT are used, and how are errors surfaced back to business users?

7. Billing creation technicals
For /SPE/CREATE_NEW_BILLING_CALL, which posting mode / error logging approach is used, and how do you ensure idempotency (e.g., check delivery billing status FKSTK to avoid duplicate billing on retries)?  For errors like Account Determination in billing, how it has been addressed?

8. MV45AFZZ guardrails
Beyond the “workflow user” and exclusion flag, what additional guards in MV45AFZZ (USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE) do you apply (e.g., credit status, incompletion status) before setting TVAK LISOF for automation?

9. Master data completeness
Which Incompletion Procedures (header/item/schedule line) are assigned to order/delivery/billing to block automation until mandatory data (partner roles, pricing/tax data, shipping data) is complete?

entry.title: Re: How to transport Native mobile app create using BTP S...
entry.content: SAP has introduced SAP Content Agent + SAP Cloud Transport Management (CTMS) as the recommended and supported way to transport Native / MDK / Mobile Services mobile apps across BTP subaccounts (DEV → TEST → PROD).

entry.title: Re: Print Preview from RAP Action with v4 OData and Multiple Selection
entry.content: Same concern as Prasanna, this looks like AI-generated code, it's not clear how the front end captures the data and generates the print preview.

entry.title: Re: Deactivate 4G group valuation ledger with 5w2
entry.content:
Good day @s0016807163 

Thank you for your question.

No, you cannot deactivate ledger 4G once you have activated 5W2. Ledger scoping belongs to an initial decision you have to make, and once you have activated them you cannot remove them afterward.

See the following reference for more insights.

3530183 - Assigning Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Details on Configuration in General Ledger Accounting and Asset Accounting - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/60e06e02b32c43f0adcc38c540c384d0.html?locale=en-US

3401017 - FAQs about S/4HANA Public Cloud System Reprovisioning - SAP for Me

I suggest you check Assign Accounting Principle to Ledger and Company Codes and make sure your Ledger 4G has a valid consistent accounting principle assignment, because the system checks require all ledgers to be configured correctly before postings are allowed.

In your case, since your DEV Customising system is still being provisioned, the safest sequence I recommend is:

  1. Check whether any postings already exist in the system.
  2. If no postings exist yet, then complete the configuration for 4G in activity 103556 and bring the ledger model into a consistent state.
  3. If your business confirms that 5W2 was activated by mistake and 4G is not wanted, raise the reprovisioning discussion immediately before more configuration and testing continue.

See the following reference for more insights.

3726924 - Pre Scoping Questionnaire – Finance Design Decisions (Mandatory to Avoid Reprovisioning) - SAP for Me

Here is an insightful Blog: In-Depth with SAP S/4HANA – Ledger Scoping Q&A

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Batch Determination Enhancement with Shelf-Life Fresh...
entry.content:
1. Extend Business Partner Master
Create a custom field in the Business Partner master (via Custom Fields and Logic app in S/4HANA Public Cloud). This field will hold the customer channel (e.g., MT, E-commerce, CSD, GT, FS, B2B). Assign the field to the BP role relevant for sales so it’s available during order processing.

2. Use the Field in Batch Search Strategy Determination
In batch search strategy customizing, define condition records that reference the customer channel field. For MT, E-commerce, and CSD → assign the freshness-based strategy. For GT, FS, B2B → assign the FIFO strategy. This way, the system automatically picks the right determination logic based on the BP channel.

3. Enforce Minimum Freshness Threshold
Create a characteristic for freshness percentage (calculated from shelf-life data). In the selection class, add this characteristic with a restriction (≥ 75%). Maintain the threshold value in a configuration table or SSCUI setting so administrators can adjust it without development. During sales order batch determination, only batches meeting the threshold are proposed.

4. Restrict to Domestic Orders
Use sales area / country fields in condition records to limit the freshness-based strategy to domestic transactions. Export orders and branch transfers will bypass this logic and continue with standard FIFO.

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Wien - 09.04.2026
entry.content:
Kapazität: maximal 10 Personen

Ein gemütlicher Abend in lockerer Runde - zum zwanglosen Austausch über Themen rund um SAP und mehr.
Von der SAP Community, für die SAP Community

Tritt der Gruppe Wien bei und subscribe den Blog-Post SAP Stammtisch Wien, um über zukünftige Events informiert zu werden.

entry.title: Re: Is there a BADI that is called before the workitem is...
entry.content:
Hi,

BAdI /IWWRK/BADI_WF_BEFORE_UPD_IB allows to handle decision runtime in the following cases (after my inbox action):

- step type for the workflow task is not User Decision,
- to execute an action before the work item is updated with the user decision.

So does not fit to your business need. Technical point of view the possible options are depend on the used workflow type and the required change. See details about the available extensibility options in Community page:
SAP Business Workflow and flexible workflow extensibility via Business Add Inns


BR
Laszlo

entry.title: I am INAGANTY NABI SAHEB. I am pursuing final year B.Tech in CIET College.
entry.content: I’m currently working on an SAP PP (Production Planning) Implementation Project where I am gaining hands-on experience in production and manufacturing processes. Through this project, I am learning how real business requirements are analyzed and configured in SAP, including areas like Bill of Materials (BOM), Routing, Work Centers, Production Planning, and Material Requirements Planning (MRP). This experience is helping me understand how organizations plan, manage, and execute their production processes efficiently, ensuring optimal utilization of resources and timely delivery of products. I am also gaining insights into shop floor control, production orders, and capacity planning within SAP systems. I’m passionate about continuously improving my knowledge in SAP and understanding how technology supports efficient production planning and operational decision-making in organizations.

entry.title: Why Now Is the Time to Rethink Your ERP: Understanding SAP Cloud ERP and the Power of a Fresh Start
entry.content:
For many organizations, SAP ERP has been the backbone of business operations for years, sometimes decades. These systems have supported growth and stability, but they were implemented in a different era of business needs, regulatory requirements, and technological possibilities.

Today, the pace of change has accelerated dramatically. New compliance demands, rapid market shifts, AI-driven work models, and global expansion requirements are stretching older SAP ERP releases beyond what they were originally designed to support.

The mounting pressure to modernize

Customers running older SAP ERP releases increasingly face challenges such as:

  • Growing operational risk from aging, highly customized systems
  • Manual workarounds and resource drain, limiting IT’s ability to innovate
  • Technical debt that makes upgrades costly and complex
  • Rapidly approaching maintenance deadlines, increasing urgency to act
  • Missed opportunities for automation and AI

In short: maintaining the status quo is becoming more complex and more expensive.

Why more companies are considering a fresh start with cloud ERP?

Modern cloud ERP isn’t just a new deployment option; it’s a new operating model. It delivers simplicity, continuous innovation, and the ability to scale without the burden of heavy custom code or technical upgrades. Across industries, organizations are seeing value in five key areas:

  1. Agility and scalability
    Adopt best‑practice processes, respond faster to change, and scale operations globally without infrastructure constraints.
  2. Cost efficiency and predictable operations
    Shift from hardware ownership and upgrade projects to a subscription model with predictable spend.
  3. Continuous innovation
    Receive new capabilities, including embedded analytics, automation, and AI, through automated update cycles multiple times per year.
  4. Strong security and resilience
    Benefit from enterprise‑grade security, global SLAs, and always‑current compliance delivered by SAP.
  5. Faster time to value
    With a fit‑to‑standard approach and ready‑to‑run best practices, organizations achieve results quickly reducing implementation effort and accelerating ROI.

A Cloud ERP built for existing SAP customers

SAP Cloud ERP helps companies running SAP ERP on-premise to move forward with confidence:

  • Run in the cloud with a modern, intuitive user experience, embedded intelligence, and continuous updates
  • Get innovation moving with global localization coverage, seamless extensibility, and rapid delivery of new features
  • Move with confidence using SAP Activate, migration accelerators, and tools like the Data Migration Cockpit, reducing complexity while preserving familiar structures and terminology

Many customers choose to start fresh, leaving behind outdated custom code and organizational complexity while keeping the essentials that still provide value.

Real outcomes from real customers

Companies adopting SAP Cloud ERP report outcomes such as:

  • Faster financial close cycles
  • Reduced customization footprint
  • Increased automation and visibility
  • Improved user satisfaction
  • Lower operational and infrastructure costs
  • Faster onboarding of subsidiaries and acquisitions

Most importantly, they gain the ability to innovate continuously, without waiting for the next big upgrade.

 

Ready to understand what Cloud ERP really means for your organization?

If you’re running an older SAP ERP release, now is the right moment to explore what a modern cloud ERP could unlock for your business, especially with time‑to‑value becoming a critical decision factor.

We invite you to join our upcoming webinar:

Understanding SAP Cloud ERP: Public Cloud Concepts & Operating Model

You’ll learn:

  • Why organizations running older SAP ERP systems are rethinking their ERP strategy
  • What “public cloud ERP” really means in practice
  • How a SaaS operating model delivers continuous innovation
  • How SAP supports a smooth transition through proven tools and methodology
  • What a fresh‑start journey can look like for both business and IT teams

The session gives you a clear, practical foundation without spoiling the deeper insights we’ll cover live.

Register here: https://events.sap.com/transformation/en_us/home.html

entry.title: Integrated Toolchain: The Combined View LeanIX + SAP Signavio + SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
This Blog is a recap of the first three integrations  LeanIX + SAP Signavio + SAP Cloud ALM and a full end-to-end demo showing what the connected toolchain looks like in practice. Bookmark it as your go-to reference for all things Integrated Toolchain

Blogs 1 to 3 each activated one integration in the SAP Integrated Toolchain. If you haven't read them yet, here is where to start

  • Start here : Why the SAP Integrated Toolchain Is the Future of Digital Transformation   

Then Begin:

  • Blog 1 :  Integrated Toolchain: LeanIX + SAP Cloud ALM: Auto-Discovering Your SAP Landscape
  • Blog 2 :  LeanIX + SAP Signavio: Connecting Your Application Portfolio to Your Business Processes 
  • Blog 3 :  SAP Signavio + SAP Cloud ALM: Connecting Process Design to Implementation

Further Important Reading:

  • Bridging Strategy to Execution: How LeanIX, Signavio, and WalkMe Create the Ultimate EA Toolchain
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with SAP Signavio in SAP Landscapes
  • How Enterprise Architects Can Unlock Strategic Value with WalkMe in SAP Landscapes

'SAP Integrated toolchain Demo"

 

Step-by-Step Guidance and Best Practices

In this section you may find the step-by-step guidance and best practices on how the toolset works seamlessly together to deliver business value.

The following documentation link on SAP Help is your go-to-place on how to run ERP Transformation leveraging the integrated toolchain: SAP ERP Transformation with integrated toolchain.

Some highlights what you can find in the documentation:

  • Organizational Setup for Transformation
  • Harmonized Meta Model, Technical Setup, and Integrations
    • Single-Sign-On Setup using SAP Identity Authentication Service
    • SAP LeanIX and SAP Cloud ALM Integration
    • SAP LeanIX and SAP Signavio Integration
    • SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM Integration
    • Tricentis Tosca Cloud - SAP Cloud ALM Integration
    • WalkMe and SAP Signavio for Process-Led Adoption
  • Governance and Operating Model
  • Step-by-Step Guide for Process-Driven Transformation with SAP's Integrated Toolchain
  • Step-by-Step Guide for System Conversion with Integrated Toolchain
  • RISE with SAP Methodology

Live Trainings on Learning Hub

  • Managing Your SAP S/4HANA Transformation, Leveraging the Integrated Toolchain
  • Hands-on bootcamp: Leveraging the integrated toolchain for business transformation
  • Hands-on Bootcamp: Leveraging the integrated toolchain for system conversion
  • Navigating Transformations with the Integrated Toolchain

e-Learning Material

  • Navigating RISE with SAP Transformations Leveraging the Integrated Toolchain
  • Building the foundation for Business Transformation - Customer webcast available on demand

Certification

  • SAP LeanIX
  • SAP Signavio
  • SAP CloudALM

  +   +

entry.title: Re: Capacity responsible planner group in work center
entry.content:
Good day @hamza07 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your description, the issue you face does not look like a business user issue inside the work center app itself. The root issue is that Capacity Responsibility is mandatory, but in your Test tenant the value help is empty, so the work center cannot be created. The first check you should carry out is in Change Work Center under the Capabilities tab to verify the capacity header setup and which version you have activated.

Consult these references for more details.

3602404 - Error when Clicking the Edit Option in the Manage Work Center Capacity App - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2dfa044a255f49e89a3050daf3c61c11/428b6b7d1f4041ab84756d38665605d8.html?locale=en-US

Since you already confirmed that the entry exists in Customizing but not in your Test tenant, I suggest you compare both tenant (confirm whether you moved the relevant configuration between Customizing and Test) and if necessary, raise an incident with SAP so they can verify the missing configuration/expert configuration content in your Test tenant.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Accessing and Calling Existing BRF+ Functions in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I would like to use functions from an existing BRF+ application within a developer extensibility scenario in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Using the app "Simplified BRF+ Workbench – Extensibility" (App ID: FDT_WD_WORKBENCH_SIMPLIFIED / FDT_WD_WB_SIMPLIFIED_EXT_AC), I was able to locate the application "OPD WM Handling Unit".

However, when attempting to display the application or one of its functions, I receive the following error message:

"You have no authorization to display object OPD WM Handling Unit"

I assume that the function IDs of standard BRF+ objects are stable and identical across systems, rather than being system-generated, so that they can be used reliably in custom code. That's why my goal is to retrieve the function IDs so that I can call the corresponding BRF+ functions from an ABAP class using CL_FDT_FUNCTION_PROCESS_API.

Is it possible to obtain the necessary authorizations to display these objects in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
If not, is there an alternative way to identify and call these existing BRF+ functions from a developer extensibility scenario?

entry.title: REFX-CLM-Public Cloud-Valuation contract -Transfer postings not triggering to Cost Center
entry.content:
Hi All,

As per the business requirement in Public Cloud system while doing Contract periodic postings should be triggered to a cost center with integrated/transfer postings.

As i checked in Public cloud configuration at Condition types Distribution Formula setting have only YA-Divide Equally, generally we can see that distribution postings dropdown will have a multiple option like Object also. 

While creating a valuation contract at condition tab we can choose option for distribution object as a Cost Center under Distribution but when we assign valuation rule for Contract Object then Distribution tab will disappear and valuation tab will enable.

So when we tried Periodic postings with Integrated/Transfer postings system is not picking up the cost center since distribution object is not available.

Tried by creating a settlement rule at contract level as Account assignment category as a cost center but did not find any app to execute the CO settlement rule for this as we can see in on-premise system.

Dear experts any solution to reach above requirement. please advise.

Thanks in advance and appreciate your help.

Regards,

CM

entry.title: Re: Interim valuation for Accumulators
entry.content: Please share more info which product type you are referring. This discrepancy in TPM1 (Postings) versus the bank statement often stems from how SAP Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) distinguishes between the book value (accounting view) and the market value (bank view). When dealing with accumulators or similar financial instruments, SAP’s logic for the positive/negative sign is governed by Position Component, the Calculation Logic in the Valuation Area, and the "Direction" of the transaction. e.g., Acquisition Value, Security Valuation, FX. TPM1 will mathematically force the valuation to follow that sign convention, regardless of the market reality. Suggested Troubleshooting Steps Check T-Code TPM13: Compare the "Operational Value" with the "Book Value." This will show you exactly which component (FX, Price, or Cost) is driving the negative sign. Verify Market Data (T-Code JBAR / FW17): Ensure the price entered for the valuation date doesn't have an inverted rate. Review Configuration: Go to Assign Steps and Types in the Valuation settings. Check if the Reset indicator is flagged. If a previous month had a large negative valuation that wasn't reset, it could be distorting the current period's delta.

entry.title: Disable Credit Check in Custom Sales Process
entry.content:
Background

As shared in a previous blog, it is possible to create custom sales order types in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. This new innovation provides greater flexibility to fulfill customer business requirements in the Order‑to‑Cash process.
In this blog, I will share a real business use case and explain how to utilize custom sales order types to address customer requirements.

Customer Requirement

Supply company XYZ sells various products to customers. For certain customers, the credit check is required when selling high‑value products to ensure that sufficient credit limits are available before processing the sales order. However, for low‑value products, a credit check is not required in order to reduce the additional operational effort involved in order processing. The company XYZ also wants to have reporting to analyze sales data of these sales processes. 

Since only the standard sales order type “OR” is available to support the sell‑from‑stock process, a single sales order type is not sufficient to differentiate between sales processes for high‑value products and low‑value products. In addition, the credit check configuration is maintained at the sales document type level. Therefore, having different sales order types is a logical approach to support these distinct business use cases.

Solution 

Let's see how custom sales order type fulfills this requirement. 

Assume you are on SAP CBC and already finished the activity "Define Scope" and "Set Up Organization Structure". Now you can open the activity "Configure Standard Order Type" to define a new custom sales order type. 

Create a custom sales order type by copying standard type template XTA. 

Save and confirm the custom sales order type. Then deploy new custom sales order types to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Wait until the deployment is complete. 

Once custom sales order types are deployed to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can open the activity "Configure Your Business Processes", and search for SSCUI 102137 "Set Credit Limit Checks for Sales Document Types".

System will navigate to the SSCUI in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to configure the credit limit checks for sales document type. Find the entry of custom sales order type ZCRD and set the setting "Check Credit" to empty, which means no credit check. 

Save the configuration in SSCUI, and now the credit check is disabled for custom sales order type ZCRD. 

Log on with user "Internal Sales Representative" and open the app "Create Sales Order" to create a sales order with custom sales order type ZCRD. 

The sold-to party 10100005 is the customer who has credit limit maintained in the master data. 

Add the sales order item and save the sales order. 

Once sales order is saved, go to tab "Status and Blocks" and you will see this sales order is not blocked by credit check.

You can continue creating outbound delivery from the sales orders with custom sales order type ZCRD because no credit check is performed for such sales orders. 

Let's compare with the standard sales order type "OR". Create a sales order with sales order type "OR".

Use same sold-to party "10100005".

Add the item and save the sales order. A popup message is shown that credit limit is exceeded.

Go to tab "Status and Blocks", you will see the sales order with standard order type OR is blocked by credit check.

You need to work with Credit Controller to release the DCD (Documented Credit Decision) to get rid of the credit block on sales order to proceed the subsequent logistics processes. 

Conclusion

The introduction of custom sales order types is a significant innovation that greatly enhances flexibility in the Order‑to‑Cash process. It allows customers to define their own sales processes with minimal configuration effort to support different sales scenarios and process patterns.

With a single click, all relevant configurations from the standard sales order type “OR” are copied to the new custom sales order type, enabling customers to immediately execute the sell‑from‑stock process without any additional fine‑tuning. This capability empowers customers to adapt their sales processes more efficiently while reducing implementation complexity.

Please stay tuned for more template copy use cases, and feel free to submit your requirements through the Customer Influence Portal to help us prioritize our product roadmap.

entry.title: Re: G/L Account Short Description showing On Inputs and O...
entry.content: You will need to use Custom Fields and Logic or CDS views to bring the long description into the journal entry and line item apps. The standard system only shows short descriptions, so extension is the way to meet your requirement.

entry.title: Re: Building Low Code Extensions with Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA and SAP Build
entry.content:
Great article, really liked how you explained the combination of key user extensibility with SAP Build in a practical way.

The point about empowering business users to create low-code extensions without heavy dependency on IT is especially relevant. It clearly reduces communication gaps and speeds up innovation cycles, which is something many organizations struggle with.

I also found the distinction between in-app (local) extensions and side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP very useful. Knowing when to use each approach is critical for maintaining a clean and scalable architecture.

Overall, a very insightful write-up on how organizations can leverage low-code capabilities in SAP S/4HANA to balance flexibility and governance. Looking forward to more such practical examples.

entry.title: Re: Optional Task: Use Joule CDS Unit Test Generation
entry.content: Package : ZAC_148863

entry.title: Re: How to access Environment Variables in Script Task/BP...
entry.content:
Hi Abhishek,

Environment Variables unfortunately cannot be accessed directly inside a Script Task in SAP Build Process Automation.
They are only resolved during deployment, so Script Tasks do not have a way to read them at runtime.

Recommended Approach:

Instead of using Environment Variables directly, you should:

  1. Create a Process Input Parameter
  2. Map your Environment Variable to Process Input Parameter during workflow deployment
  3. Access the parameter inside the Script Task via $.context.processInputs
    Hope this helps you — thanks!

entry.title: Re: Guide: Step By Step Procedure To Send And Receive Messages in a SAP Event Mesh Instance
entry.content:
Hi @sanj22,

you need to check the service descriptor of your messaging client. Did you allow to publish/consume the queue that you are trying to access? Maybe it is in a different namespace or you didn't allow it at all.

Regards,
Tobias

entry.title: questions about x-csrf-token for SAP OData APIs
entry.content:
I am integrating SAP APIs on the Feishu Integration Platform, and my SAP system version is S/4HANA Cloud. I have encountered several issues regarding the x-csrf-token for SAP OData APIs:
  1. According to the SAP website, the validity period of the token is 30 minutes. However, in Postman, the same token can be used for an entire afternoon without renewal, and all POST requests are successful.
  2. When sending multiple GET requests to fetch the x-csrf-token in Postman, the token remains unchanged (the same string). But when sending multiple GET requests via the Feishu Integration Platform, the token is renewed every time.
  3. After obtaining the x-csrf-token via GET on the Feishu Integration Platform, subsequent POST requests throw errors.
 
My questions are:
  1. What is the renewal mechanism of the x-csrf-token returned by SAP?
  2. Can I view inbound communication logs on the SAP website to facilitate debugging?
  3. For SAP OData API POST requests, is there a solution that does not require the x-csrf-token? The Feishu Integration Platform cannot implement the x-csrf-token logic.

entry.title: Re: Variance Option Missing in Optimized Story Bar Chart
entry.content:
@N1kh1l 

No, it wasn’t showing. But I finally got it working.

To add a variance, the chart needs to include a Time Calculation—only then does the variance option become available as an add‑on.

Greetings

entry.title: SAP Cloud Identity Services - Overview, Strategy and Roadmap
entry.content:
Identity is the backbone of every digital enterprise. In this session, discover how SAP Cloud Identity Services enable secure and seamless identity and access management across SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and connected SAP solutions.
We’ll explore SAP’s IAM strategy and how it brings together identity lifecycle management, authentication, single sign-on, and authorization management into a unified approach. You’ll also get a look at the roadmap, including upcoming innovations such as SAP-managed integration for SAP S/4HANA Cloud editions and support for emerging Agentic AI scenarios.

Join us to learn how SAP Cloud Identity Services help organizations strengthen security, simplify identity management, and support the next generation of intelligent applications. 🔐

Register

Live on YouTube

entry.title: Re: #10 - Review Cutover Plan - And The Importance for a Successful Go-Live
entry.content:
Great article , this really highlights how critical the cutover phase is for a successful go-live.

I particularly liked how you broke down the structure of pre-cutover, execution, and post-cutover activities. In real projects, many teams focus heavily on build and testing, but underestimate how important a well-defined cutover plan is.

From what I’ve seen, the biggest value of a strong cutover plan is in minimizing business disruption and ensuring data integrity during transition. Without clear timelines, ownership, and contingency planning, even a well-tested system can face issues at go-live.

Also, the emphasis on roles and responsibilities is very relevant cutover is a highly coordinated effort, and clarity here makes a big difference in execution.

Overall, a very practical and insightful write-up for anyone involved in SAP implementations. Looking forward to more such content!

entry.title: Re: element with types abap.dats and abap.dec optional in...
entry.content: The odata Version is V4

entry.title: SAP Business AI in Public Sector - Partner live expert session
entry.content:
Public sector organizations are under pressure to do more with fewer resources, higher expectations, and constant change.  The opportunity?  AI that actually delivers impact.

In our upcoming SAP Business AI in Public Sector – Partner live expert session, we’ll walk through how you can help customers become more efficient, more responsive, and honestly, more future-ready.

We’ll start with a clear snapshot of the public sector landscape, what’s shifting, what matters.  Then we’ll go deeper into real AI use cases, plus how to position and sell these solutions effectively (because knowing what to sell is only half the story).

There’s also a live demo on SDE 2.0, always a favorite and time for your questions.

If you’re working with public sector clients, this session can sharpen your approach and open new conversations.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/mar/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00013345.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #PublicSector #BusinessAI #DigitalTransformation #GovTech

entry.title: Secure, Compliant, and AI-Ready: The Future of Governance and Compliance on SAP BTP

Modern enterprises face increasing regulatory mandates around data protection, AI governance, customer interactions, and auditability. This session showcases how SAP BTP delivers a trusted foundation through an integrated set of compliance services—from anonymization and retention management to audit logging and AI-driven regulatory insights. Using a multi-industry demo scenario, participants will learn how to embed automated compliance into digital processes, reduce operational risk, accelerate audits, and safely unlock data for innovation and AI. Ideal for architects, security teams, product managers, and compliance stakeholders.

 

Register

Live on YouTube
